# 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka

![LastUpdated](https://img.shields.io/badge/last_updated-2026--05--06_09:44:18-green)
[![RSS](https://img.shields.io/badge/RSS-Feed-orange)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/refs/heads/main/rss.xml)
[![Broadsheet](https://img.shields.io/badge/Broadsheet-View-darkred)](https://nuuuwan.github.io/lk_news_digest)

*Generated by gpt-5 from 161 English News Articles published between 2026-04-29 and 2026-05-06.*

## 1. The ADB sharply downgraded Asia-Pacific growth and raised inflation forecasts for 2026.

The bank projected expansion of <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">4.7% this year</span> and 4.8% next year, down from 5.1% for both years, and lifted the price outlook to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">5.2% for 2026</span>. Its baseline assumed crude averaging about <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">$96 a barrel</span> in 2026, compared with $69 before the conflict period.

In Samarkand, governors discussed liquidity support and regional energy links, and Japan joined a new platform called <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">ACCEL (Action for Creating Energy Security for Long‑term Resilience)</span> to back firms and diversify power systems. A parallel update signalled that Colombo’s request for budget financing was under consideration within the lender’s internal processes.

## 2. Sri Lanka’s headline inflation jumped to 5.4% in April, led by non-food categories.

The statistics office reported a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">2.99% month‑on‑month</span> rise in the <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">CCPI (Colombo Consumer Price Index)</span>, with transport costs contributing most to the increase. Food prices rose 2.8% year‑on‑year while non‑food climbed 6.8%, and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">core inflation reached 2.8%</span>.

The monetary authority said the earlier low readings gave space to accommodate energy‑related pass‑through. It reaffirmed a medium‑term aim of keeping price gains around a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">5% objective</span>, subject to supply risks linked to global markets.

## 3. The Bar Association raised concern over a May Day remark by the President on an upcoming court decision.

The lawyers’ body said <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">public confidence</span> could be harmed if statements imply foreknowledge of judicial outcomes. A group of <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">26 opposition MPs</span> also objected, arguing that the comment risked undermining perceptions of <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">independence</span>.

At the weekly briefing, a cabinet spokesman said hearing dates were already public on the <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">court calendar</span>. He listed several matters scheduled for determination later this month.

## 4. Lawmakers were told an Insolvency and Restructuring Bill could unlock a Rs. 30 billion distressed-asset pipeline.

Witnesses said a ‘rescue‑first’ framework would allow time‑bound workouts and reduce liquidation, while cautioning that tax clarity and court capacity would shape results. The committee noted scope for <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">committee‑stage amendments</span> on fiscal treatment and reporting rules before debate.

Advisers added that aggregating capital through modern vehicles and aligning with creditor voting thresholds would improve recoveries. Industry groups flagged that <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">SMEs need realistic moratoriums</span> to match cash conversion cycles.

## 5. Sri Lanka and the Maldives exchanged seven MoUs spanning security, education, tourism and health.

The agreements were signed during a state visit marking <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">61 years of formal ties</span>, with leaders also signalling interest in fisheries processing and boat‑building. Each side allocated <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">land for diplomatic missions</span>, and a business forum was set for follow‑up dealmaking.

Talks covered <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">maritime cooperation</span> and joint climate advocacy, and universities and archives agreed on institutional links. The visiting delegation also discussed a possible <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">banking presence</span> and premium seafood market access.

## 6. The Government approved 1,000 interim homes and repair grants for places of worship damaged by Cyclone Ditwah.

Officials said nearly <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">6,100 houses were destroyed</span> and over <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">115,000 were partially damaged</span>, with several hundred families still in safe centres as of 22 April. Designs by the building research agency will guide temporary units, and grants up to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Rs. 7.5 million</span> were set for full reconstruction of religious sites.

The army was tasked with construction support, and funding caps of Rs. 5 million apply for partial repairs. A <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">rental allowance scheme</span> remained in place where housing supply permits.

## 7. Police arrested over 120 foreign nationals in Rajagiriya in a raid on alleged fraudulent operations.

Investigators seized laptops and phones from a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">multi‑storey apartment</span> site and began <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">forensic analysis</span>. Suspects from several countries were detained, and some individuals reportedly fled during the operation.

Local officers said further inquiries were underway to trace those who escaped. Statements indicated the group coordinated large‑scale <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">illegal financial activity</span>.

## 8. The Cabinet cleared rules expanding the Central Bank’s data‑collection powers with sanctions for non‑compliance.

New regulations under the <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">2023 Act</span> allow the authority to require statistics from external parties and set <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">administrative actions</span> for failure to report. The financial statements and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">operational report for 2025</span> were also tabled for parliamentary oversight.

The rules define formats, confidentiality safeguards, and entities within scope. Officials said the measures support <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">policy formulation</span> and monitoring.

## 9. A Bill to amend VAT proposed cutting the registration threshold and taxing cross‑border digital services from July 2026.

The draft lowers the annual limit to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Rs. 36 million</span> and the per‑period limit to Rs. 9 million, widening coverage to wholesale and retail. Non‑resident platforms supplying to users in the country would be required to register, with business‑to‑business transactions excluded if documentation is in order.

Levy on financial services rises to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">20.5%</span>, and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">secured POS (point‑of‑sale) systems</span> become mandatory on a timeline. Zero‑rating is clarified for garment buying offices paid in foreign currency.

## 10. Sri Lanka sent its largest business delegation to the SelectUSA Investment Summit.

Fourteen participants joined sessions focused on building links in healthcare, manufacturing and digital services. The embassy said the group aimed to form commercial partnerships and expand <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">two‑way investment</span>.

Organisers highlighted sector‑specific networking and state‑level opportunities. Prior editions have helped channel <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">foreign direct inflows</span> into US operations.

## 11. The Russian Health Minister began a three‑day official visit for talks on pharmaceuticals, equipment and training.

The delegation held meetings with counterparts and visited the <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">national hospital</span> and a medical faculty to discuss exchanges. Plans included exploring <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">academic links</span> and cooperation in areas such as Ayurveda and health tourism.

Officials also met the foreign ministry to review wider engagement. The trip followed growing attention to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">supply resilience</span> in the sector.
